column5: Supermemory's 85.2% LongMemEval score is strong, but the cloud-only model and undisclosed pricing may be deal-breakers for some teams. Consider Hypabase Supermemory casts a wide net across data sources but still relies on ad-hoc fact extraction under the hood. Mem0 offers the broadest community but scores lowest on retrieval benchmarks. Hypabase takes a different path: precise role-based retrieval powered by a formal linguistic framework, so queries like "when is Sarah's meeting?" return exactly the right hyperedge—not a list of loosely related facts to sift through. Factor Mem0 Supermemory Hypabase LLM-based, ad- Hybrid RAG + fact AMR (formal linguistic Extraction hoc extraction framework) Representation Triples Vector + extracted facts N-ary hyperedges LongMemEval* 49% 85.2% 87.4% Personalization — — 100% AMR Extraction + Hyperedges Hypabase uses Abstract Meaning Representation (AMR)—a formal framework from computational linguistics—to extract structured facts in PENMAN notation with karaka semantic roles (from Panini's Sanskrit grammar): "Sarah scheduled a meeting with the team for 3pm Thursday"
